action.reasoning: Observation: val_eval on same-domain val split shows model always outputs identical <box>425,141,551,444</box> (rescaled ~[1428,296,1851,932]) for all instructions on same screenshot (mean_iou=0.0, grounding_score=0.0); test score 0.0 with 1 prior 0.0006 best. Train reaches low loss (~0.28) confirming format learning but no instruction/image conditioning. Analysis: truncated/incomplete train.py (cut at loss logging, no merge/save in current workspace version) + low-res training + possible prompt_len masking fragility on variable patches caused degenerate predictions; domain gap + detail loss from 512px cap primary culprits per prior diagnostics. Complete non-truncated code, random sampling per step, higher max_pixels=768*28*28 for UI details, merge_and_unload for reliable eval load, adjusted r=16/lr=2e-5/2000 steps (~25min) fits remaining ~1h. Predict completed with full JSON/clip/rescale/parsers matching contract. This should yield non-zero score before budget exhaustion. Decision: overwrite train.py+predict.py with full robust versions, execute train then submit_eval on resulting merged checkpoint.
